entry.title: Re: how to remove decimal places from field type number i...
entry.content: Hello @thiago Eva , thanks for your answer the "number format" does not exist in manage templates > job requisition, it only exists in manage templates > job application

entry.title: Re: Get technical information from Service Definition using ABAP
entry.content:
Hello Ekalyvas,

as fas as I know, there is no such a standard FM or class, because calling metadata is the standard way for such services ( and not only in SAP world). But there are tools in abap for converting the results of the metadata call to convert them to abap tables/strucutres. ( e.g. XML transformation etc..)

entry.title: SAP BTP - Oauth tab/section is missing under security tab?
entry.content: Oauth section under security Tab in SAP BTP is missing. Have all authorizations but don't see Oauth section.

entry.content: Is there other customers impacted on DC33 datacenter by these Home Page error ?

entry.title: Re: how to remove decimal places from field type number i...
entry.content: Hi user116, sorry for the confusion, I'll remove the answer, and if I find some other alternative I'll let you know!

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere Object Store activation in partner tenant
entry.content:
Hello,

before opening the ticket I found that our tenant has feature flag DWCO_LARGE_SYSTEMS = true

So I do not understand if the system is already enabled or not ( I do not have the tenant configuration tab so I can not see if there is an object store attached).

From space management I can not add a spaced based on "file" so I think is not enabled.

Do you confirm that the procedure is only opening a ticket with "This ticket is a service request for enablement of feature toggle "DWCO_LARGE_SYSTEMS" in Datasphere Tenant ..." ?

Thank you in advance

Best Regards,

Alberto

entry.title: Re: H1_2025 P4 | Manage Pending Hire RBP permission issue
entry.content: solved last friday by SAP support thanks to script execution at instance level

entry.title: How to fix time out issues where tracking won't allow user to complete?
entry.content:
SAP Success Factors LMS. (latest version)
SCORM 1.2 uploaded/generated from Articulate 365. (latest version)

Training packages works for multiple users/across multiple accounts. However, some users experience inability to complete training(s) after timing out their session. (Can find no issues in the trainings themselves, tested across multiple browsers and multiple systems with no issues. This seems to be a random issue and does not seem to be narrowed in on one user or even one training.  The only common point seems to be that they either multi-task away from the LMS session browser window or let their training sit open overnight so it times out...consequently they are able to finish the training in the buffered window/session - but it doesn't track properly and won't officially complete for them in the LMS backend. 

Wondering if there is a way to mitigate/avoid this? Perhaps an auto log-out feature/setting? (does one even exist?)

entry.title: Re: How to debug an error message during S/4 HANA Billing document creation(VF01)
entry.content: Very helpful post! Thank you for the sharing!

entry.title: Re: DC33 | Home Page Error | Looks like something went wrong

entry.title: Re: DC33 | Home Page Error | Looks like something went wrong
entry.content:
Yes, all instances from DC33 are affected: performancemanager5 + hcm12preview.

Hope to be solved soon.

entry.title: ABAP Proxy Transfoms ABAP Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

In S/4HANA On Premise/Private Cloud to transform an abap proxy into xml, and vice versa, we can use the CL_PROXY_XML_TRANSFORM class.

Unfortunately this class is not released for use in the cloud.

Is there a class in S/4HANA Public Cloud, which has the same functionality?

If not, can you suggest an alternative way for the transformation?

Thanks in advance.

Best Regards.

Marco Spera

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Escape House for May - ABAP Cloud
entry.content:
Escaped! It was fun. Learned some bits here and there in this journey.

Thanks for such challenges.

entry.title: Re: Ad hoc Query/Report Creation in SAP
entry.content:
EPI-Use has Query manager that works well with SAP HCM tables.

I like SAP SQ01 ad hoc query; you still need to know the tables and joins.

If the CDS views are already built, SAP Analysis Office is good to work with.

Paul

entry.title: Multiple Billing Document Attachments in Single Email Transmission
entry.content:
Good afternoon,

Is it possible to group multiple Billing Documents (PDF Attachments) for the same customer in a single email output transmission?

entry.title: FIN_ACDOC_ITEM_SUBSTITUTION
entry.content:
On using loop condition as suggested by Google Gemini to prepare the code for BAdI FIN_ACDOC_ITEM_SUBSTITUTION for performing profit center substitution for intercompany supplier invoice journal entries as requested by our client, we are unable to get it to work as it reads such journal entries line by line. Due to the restrictions of this BAdI and the cloud environment, it is not working as expected. SAP has confirmed this restriction of the BAdI that when it operates in context of one item, the other items of posting can't be accessed.

We are getting a ODATA runtime error: 'TSV_TNEW_PAGE_ALLOC_FAILED' while using the loop condition. We are also getting similar runtime errors while performing other functions like PGI for outbound delivery, MIGO post goods receipt etc.

 

Also conditions like class and method can't be used for coding due to non availability of these keywords in the cloud environment

entry.title: Re: Fixed Assets Migration - Asset Value Date and Depreciation calculation Issue
entry.content: Did you only fill out the Cumulative Values worksheet (which is as of the end of the previous year) or the cumulative values and Posted Values (which is for transactions in the current year)?

entry.title: Re: Canada Compliance forms for remote employee's Province
entry.content:
Hi Raj_SF,

You can explore the Meta Data Override functionality under Manage Data. The compliance forms are assigned to the new hire based on the country and state of the work location so the Federal Forms will trigger correctly, but since the workstate value is used for the provincial forms, you will have to confirm if this is correctly maintained for each employee. If work location isn’t available, the country of the legal entity location (organization's location) is considered which will not work for you. If neither of these locations are available, no forms will appear.

For the remote locations you might have to create some mechanism to maintain the correct values for each employee and then guide the system to pull the Work State value from a different field in the system. We have done this via a Primary Work Location (Remote Location) field under Job Information. This can then be filled via business rules or during the Review New Hire Data (RNHD) step. This object contains all the relevant location information. Be cautious when you consider this approach and only use it if the RNHD step is always used. Since Job Information fields are not exposed to Onboardees, the system will have no step to populate the required values if the RNHD step is skipped. Compliance forms are triggered after the Personal Data Collection (PDC) step and if the values aren't available by then, no provincial forms will trigger.

Find below example override code where custom-string12 (Primary Work Location) under Job Information is used to resolve the remote locations. This field references a custom object cust_primaryWorkLocation that contains a province field cust_province. We then asking the system to pull the workstate value from this object.

 

workcountry
Prepopulated Employee Central Fields Metadata
 
{
"entityPath": "jobInfoNav",
"field": "countryOfCompany"
}
workstate
Prepopulated Employee Central Fields Metadata
 
{
"entityPath":"jobInfoNav/customString12Nav/cust_provinceNav",
"field": "externalCode"
}

  Also consider permissions. Your OnboardingBpeAdmin user will require access to these fields as this is the system user associated to the compliance processes.

Hope something in here will help you to find a solution that can work for you.

Regards

Frans

entry.title: Re: Job Application still shows on home page however JR c...
entry.content:
Hi @HeenaG ,

In SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting, when a job requisition (JR) is closed and a candidate has already been hired, but the application still appears on the candidate's home page with only the "Withdraw Application" option, this is usually due to the application status not being properly dispositioned or not marked as completed in the recruiting system

Steps to Remove the Job Application from the Candidate's Home Page:

1. Check the Application Status
Navigate to the candidate's application for the closed requisition.

Confirm the current status (e.g., "Interview", "Offer", "Hired", etc.).

If the candidate is not dispositioned correctly, the system will still show the application as "active" on their homepage.

2. Disposition the Candidate
Go to Manage Requisitions > Candidates for that job requisition.

Locate the candidate whose application is still showing.

Move the candidate to a final status (e.g., "Hired", "Rejected", or a custom final status).

Ensure this status is marked as a disqualification status or end-of-process status in the status set configuration.

3. Mark as Completed
In some configurations, even after moving to a final status, the system requires the application to be marked as completed.

Go to Candidate Summary > Application, and see if there's an option to "Mark as Completed".

4. Check Recruiting Settings
Go to Admin Center > Manage Recruiting Settings.

Look for a setting related to "Hide completed applications from the candidate's home page" or "Automatically archive completed applications".

Make sure this is enabled if you want closed/hired applications to be hidden.

5. Review Permission Roles
Ensure the candidate’s role doesn't have special visibility into closed requisitions.

Go to Admin Center > Manage Permission Roles > Candidate Role, and review the permissions related to application visibility.

Optional: Scheduled Job to Archive Applications
If applications need to be removed after a period of time, ensure there’s a Scheduled Job (Job Scheduler) set up to archive or purge old applications.

Check under Admin Center > Scheduled Job Manager or work with your system admin/consultant.

Notes:
Applications only disappear from the candidate home page when they are in a final and inactive state.

"Withdraw Application" will show as long as the system considers the application active and the requisition is technically still linked.

Hope this helps!! 

Thanks,

kr,

karthick.

entry.title: Re: INVOIC IDoc processing of Intercompany billing (IV)
entry.content:
Hi 

Thank you for the document. When you have a standard cost on a material and have a intercompany price that is a mark up on the cost, the need is that when the IDOC is posted, we have the accounting entry created in SAP as follows:

  1. The Purchase account is posted at the standard cost 
  2. The difference between the intercompany price and the standard cost is posted in the PPV account.

Would appreciate inputs from experts on how this has been managed where there is a legal requirement to ensure that the costs are properly recorded in the correct accounts.

entry.title: Automating Quality Control: Rework Routing Disposition in SAP Digital Manufacturing
entry.content:
Introduction

In manufacturing, every deviation from quality standards can cascade issues across the supply chain, increase costs, and risk customer satisfaction. SAP Digital Manufacturing helps manufacturers proactively manage such deviations by automating the routing of defective parts to rework processes immediately upon detecting out-of-limit conditions during data collection on the shop floor.

Data Collection capability of SAP Digital Manufacturing enable workers to collect product and production process-related data against Shop Floor Controls (aka SFCs) in Production Operator Dashboards (aka PODs). It can also validate the collected data against predefined limits (minimum and maximum thresholds values) and ensure that all required data has been collected, for example.

When a collected Data Collection parameter is out of min/max thresholds, the system can automatically initiate the logging of a nonconformance, which can automatically disposition the defective part to a rework routing.

The Business Problem

Imagine a production line where operators collect dimensional or performance data during assembly. A single deviation beyond the acceptable range may go unnoticed until a downstream failure or audit occurs. This leads to costly scrap, delayed deliveries, and quality compliance risks.

Traditionally, rework initiation involves manual intervention: the operator spots the issue, logs a nonconformance, and a quality engineer reviews and routes the part. This delay can be costly.

Automatic Rework Disposition

SAP Digital Manufacturing introduces a proactive and automated quality safeguard: if any data point collected during production is outside predefined min/max thresholds, the system automatically logs a nonconformance and routes the part to a predefined rework path. This uses standard functionality including:

  • Data Collection Groups: Used to capture parameters such as torque, temperature, or dimensions.

  • Thresholds with Auto Logging: Define acceptable limits. When breached, SAP DM logs a nonconformance.

  • Rework Routing: Predefined alternative paths to fix issues without restarting the entire process.

Business Benefits

1. Real-time Quality Control
Operators no longer need to decide what to do when data falls outside limits. The system ensures immediate action, minimizing delays and human error.

2. Reduced Scrap and Rework Costs
By catching issues at the point of data entry and rerouting them instantly, defective products are reworked early, avoiding scrap and reducing production losses.

3. Compliance and Traceability
All events, including out-of-limit detections, nonconformance logging, disposition and rework routing execution are recorded, supporting audits, regulatory compliance, and root cause analysis.

4. Workforce Efficiency
This approach frees operators to focus on value-adding activities, while the system ensures adherence to quality procedures.

Use Case: Automotive Assembly Line

An automotive manufacturer measures torque for each fastener during chassis assembly. SAP Digital Manufacturing records each torque value. If any value exceeds defined safety thresholds, the system logs a “Torque Out of Limit” nonconformance and routes the part to a rework station for reassembly. No manual steps, no delay. This ensures safety-critical parts are verified and corrected before leaving the line.

Demo

Conclusion

This set of features together show SAP Digital Manufacturing’s vision of intelligent, automated, and integrated manufacturing operations. By empowering quality enforcement at the data collection point, it not only improves production efficiency but also helps ensure that only conforming products move forward, every time.

If you're exploring ways to strengthen your quality process without adding overhead, automated rework routing in SAP Digital Manufacturing is a simple but high-impact enhancement worth considering.

 

Do you like this post? Please let me know in the comments section what you think. Any feedback is highly appreciated.
Or, if you have any questions, please check SAP Community Q&A Area, or comment down below.

Thanks,
Manoel Costa

entry.title: Re: Data is showing in report preview but the report exported has no data - LMS
entry.content: This is common and likely due to PRD settings, check out this post: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-successfactors-learning-user-group-discussions/prd-query-doesn-t-return-anything-in-production-but-does-in-prd-linked-to/m-p/297476/emcs_t/S2h8ZW1haWx8bWVzc2FnZV9zdWJzY3JpcHRpb258TE80NkZXMkM1U1NUWVl8Mjk3NDc2fFNVQlNDUklQVElPTlN8aEs#M2259

can anyone help me out here.

 

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: I have a query I have one file in SFTP and I want to split the file based on the id .
entry.content:
I have a query I have one file in SFTP and I want to split the file based on the id

suppose the file is 

<idoc>
<header>
<sap_po>1233</sap_po>
<art_code>0003</art_code>
<name>cup coffee half cup</name>
</header>
<details>
<item>
<sap_po>1231</sap_po>
<art_code>0001</art_code>
<artname>cup tea one fit</artname>
</item>
<item>
<sap_po>1232</sap_po>
<art_code>0002</art_code>
<artname>cup drink one fit</artname>
</item>
<item>
<sap_po>1234</sap_po>
<art_code>0004</art_code>
<artname>cup drink two fit</artname>
</item>
</details>
</idoc>

 

need to create 4 files since there are four SAP_PO in the source

entry.title: Re: I have a query I have one file in SFTP and I want to split the file based on the id .
entry.content: I have designed like - SFTP- Genaral splitter - router - 4 SFTP targets

entry.title: Introducing SAP Analytics cloud Widget API
entry.content:
Great News! With the latest releases of 2025.08 for the SAP Analytics Cloud fast track customers (BI weekly release model) and for Quarterly release cycle with 2025 Q2 QRC, SAP Analytics Cloud Widget API  is now Generally available.

 

What is SAP Analytics Cloud widget API?

The SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) Widget API is a powerful tool that allows developers to embed charts from SAC stories into external applications. This blog will provide an overview of the Widget API, walk you through the integration process, and demonstrate how it can enhance your application’s analytical capabilities

Overview of the Widget API

The Widget API in SAP Analytics Cloud enables seamless embedding of SAC visualisations into external web applications, portals, or custom-built systems. With this API, you can:

  • Display interactive charts within web applications.
  • Control the content programmatically (filter, refresh, resize, etc.).
  • Ensure secure communication between your application and SAC via authentication.
  • Achieve better user experience by embedding analytics directly into Desktop and web applications.

Prerequisites

Before getting started, ensure that you have:

  • Access to SAP Analytics Cloud with the required permissions. Ex: User will be able to log in to SAP Analytics cloud and view access to stories.
  • A widgetID, storyID, TenatID that you want to embed. (Refer the section3 on how to get widget ID)
  • Basic knowledge of JavaScript and HTML.

Widget API Features

Some key capabilities of the Widget API include:

  • Embedding Charts: Integrate single or multiple charts from SAC stories.
  • Filtering and Parameters: Apply filters and programmatically to change the view.
  • Context Menu & Interactions: Charts will be interactive, and you can filter on data point selection and apply various interactive options.
  • Cross-Origin Communication: Secure communication between the parent application and SAC.

Step-by-Step Integration of SAC Charts Using Widget API

  1. Generate the Embed Link: 

In SAP Analytics Cloud:

  • Open the story in edit mode that you want to embed.
  • Navigate the widget that you want to embed and get . You’ll use this in your web application. (Refer the section3 on how to get widget ID)

      2. Set Up Your Web Application

       Here’s a simple HTML structure to integrate the SAC widget into a web page.

  3. Understanding the API Structure:

This API enables seamless embedding of widgets from SAP Analytics Cloud into external applications. It comprises three main elements: a namespace, a function call, and a set of parameters (some mandatory, some optional).

These are the elements of the API:

  • Name Space:

This defines the scope for accessing the API components. The designated namespace is:**sap.sac.api.widget**

  • Function Call:

The primary function to invoke is: **renderWidget**

  • Required Parameters:

To successfully render a widget, the following parameters must be provided:

The call is renderWidget.

  • The following parameters are mandatory:
  • Container ID
    Specifies the HTML container (such as a <div>) in your application where the widget should be embedded
  • Tenant URL
    The base URL of your SAP Analytics Cloud tenant where the widget resides.
    Example: https://sample.tenant.cloud/sap/fpa/ui
  • SAC Story ID

SAC story ID that contains the widget.

  • To retrieve the Story ID, open the story in SAC and extract it from the URL.
    Example URL: https://sample.tenant.cloud/sap/fpa/ui/app.html#/story2&/s2/C7D03902C194A72F5F130EC083762CCA

→ Story ID: C7D03902C194A72F5F130EC083762CCA

  • Widget ID

Indicates the specific widget you want to embed. In edit mode, you can find it in the Styling panel under Generic Properties.

 

In view mode, right-click on the widget, go to More Options > Copy Widget ID.

 For example, the widget ID could be chart_2.

Other Optional parameters

The following parameters are optional in the API:

  • Enable and disable the widget Interaction:

You can disable the widget interaction for the users by passing these optional parameters.

Note: By default, all these parameters are set it to true.

  • enableInteraction: true/false

With this attribute, you can enable or disable the interaction in the widget.

  • enableMenu: true/false

With this attribute, you can enable or disable the context menu for the widget.

  • showHeader: true/false

With this attribute, you can show or hide the header of the widget.

  • showFooter: true/false

With this attribute, you can show or hide the footer of the widget.

Example:

4. Authentication & Security Considerations

For secure communication, SAC requires authentication. Depending on your setup:

  • Use SAML-based SSO if your users are already authenticated in the external application.

Ensure your SAC instance and the external application are configured to allow Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) for seamless data exchange.

5.Use Case: Embedded Analytics in a Customer Portal

Imagine a scenario where a bank uses a customer portal to provide users with real-time insights into their spending patterns. By embedding SAC charts into the portal using the Widget API, the bank can:

  • Display interactive dashboards tailored to each user’s transactions.
  • Allow users to filter data by date or category directly from the portal.
  • Automatically refresh the charts when new transactions occur.

This integration enhances the user experience and keeps customers engaged with actionable insights.

Reference Application:

We have released SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint, by using SAC widget API and You can now embed the SAP Analytics Cloud story widgets into PowerPoint as an Image which can be dynamically updated with the latest Data.

7. Example: Sample webpage integrating SAC widget using widget API

  • Install Visual Studio Code

https://code.visualstudio.com/

  • Install the “Live Server” extension.

  • Open the sample html code in Visual studio code application!

  • Add SAC tenant details in the initialization displaywidget function.

  • Add the widget ID in the displaywidget function

 

  • Click the “Go Live” button to start the server and it will run the application in the browser.

  • Additionally, you can pass the filters and Variables:
  sap.sac.api.widget.renderWidget(
        containerId,
        tenant,
        storyId,
        widgetId,
        {
        filters: [{
          model: "sap.epm.Planned_Events_Sample:Planned_Events_Sample",
          values: [{
            dimension: "Date_5x2a25042z",
            value: ["026b7dcf2350c824559b8cc00612de","05aff8031d1c560fa0ea6a675cc894","53a4b48d6e946faa59c35643d775c9"],
            operation: 'in'
          }]
          },       
             {
            model:"sap.epm.Planned_Events_Sample:Planned_Events_Sample",
           values: [{
           dimension: "Event_672z323k1f",
           value: [""],
           operation: 'notIn'
          }]
        },
        {
            model:"sap.epm.Planned_Events_Sample:Planned_Events_Sample",
           values: [{
             dimension: "Date_5x2a25042z",
           value: ["026b7dcf2350c824559b8cc00612de"],
           operation: 'notIn'
          }]
        }
      ] 
      },
updatedOptions
      )
    };

  8. Troubleshooting Tips

  • Chart not loading? Ensure the SAC tenant URL is correct and accessible from the web app.
  • CORS errors? Verify that the SAC instance allows CORS from your application domain.
  • Authentication issues? Confirm the valid authentication or SSO setup is correctly configured.

  9. Further Reading & Resources

  • Enabling Iframe Embedding
  • SAP Widget API Documentation

entry.title: Integration of SAP Customer Checkout (CCO) with SAP Business ByDesign (ByD)
entry.content:
We are looking to understand if any partner or community member has implemented an integration between SAP CCO and SAP ByD capable of handling the following processes directly from the checkout:

  1. Credit Memos

    • Issuing and posting credit memos from within CCO.

    • Automatic synchronization with financial documents in ByD.

  2. Down Payments / Layaways / Partial Payments

    • Accepting partial payments (“layaway”) in CCO to reserve goods.

    • Recording partial credits and applying them automatically to open invoices in ByD.

  3. Retrieval of Customer Invoices

    • Viewing and reprinting invoices issued by ByD, accessible directly in the CCO interface.

    • Real-time validation of invoice status (paid, open, overdue).

  4. Credit Sales and Customer Credit Management

    • Enabling credit lines in CCO based on the customer profile defined in ByD.

    • Automatic blocks or alerts when a customer exceeds their credit limit.

  5. Discount Lists

    • Applying customer-specific, product-group, or campaign discounts defined in ByD but executed in CCO.

    • Competitive pricing hierarchy (percentage, fixed amount, tiered tables).

  6. Packaging/Container Management

    • Calculating and charging container deposits (e.g., beer kegs or bottles).

    • Tracking container deposits and returns directly in CCO with subsequent posting in ByD.

Questions for the Community:

  • Who has implemented any of these features between CCO and ByD?

  • Which integration tools or adapters did you use (SAP Cloud Platform Integration, CPI, OData, APIs)?

  • How did you handle simultaneous synchronization of multiple financial documents?

  • What technical challenges (performance, error handling, latency) did you face and how did you overcome them?

  • Any recommendations on functional and integration testing to ensure data consistency?

  • Can you share any blueprints or sample configuration code?

Thank you in advance for any experiences, technical suggestions, or integration code samples the community can provide.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Business Partner restrict Business Partner Roles based on Grouping at creation
entry.content:
I implemented this BADI and encountered an issue when trying to change the account group for a vendor or customer linked to a Business Partner.

When you're in Customer: General Data and click the Change Account Group button, SAP navigates to a specific function module: ACCUST for customers or ACVEND for vendors.

Therefore, you need to create these entries in the view ZBP_GROUP_ROLE. Otherwise, you’ll encounter an error when attempting to change the account group.

In my case, I was receiving a message saying that the Business Partner already exists, which didn’t clearly point me to the root cause and delayed troubleshooting.

Hope this helps!

Best regards,
Evandro

entry.title: Re: I have a query I have one file in SFTP and I want to split the file based on the id .
entry.content: <idoc> <details> <item> <sap_po>1231</sap_po> <art_code>0001</art_code> <artname>cup tea one fit</artname> </item> <item> <sap_po>1232</sap_po> <art_code>0002</art_code> <artname>cup drink one fit</artname> </item> <item> <sap_po>1234</sap_po> <art_code>0004</art_code> <artname>cup drink two fit</artname> </item> </details> </idoc>

entry.title: SAC Planning Story unable add comments in View Mode
entry.content:
Hi All,

Created Story on planning  , able to edit budget V1 values but unable to add Comments in View or Present Mode  .

Yes we can add comments  in Edit Mode . can some on suggest 

Thanks Raghu

entry.title: Meetup Fortaleza 2025
entry.content:
O objetivo do evento é reunir profissionais SAP proporcionando uma excelente oportunidade para se conectar, compartilhar experiências e discutir as ações da comunidade SAP Fortaleza.

Com uma organização planejada e conversas inspiradoras sobre inovações e soluções, o encontro promete ser um verdadeiro sucesso!

Endereço: R. Sen. Pompeu, 2610 - Benfica, Fortaleza -  Condomínio Praça da Luz - Salão de Festa

entry.title: Product Allocation Not appreaing in Availability check Sales Order
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am trying to execute the product allocation in S4 HANA sales Public cloud, and I have completed all the steps 

  1. Configure Product allocation 
  2. Manage Product Allocation Sequence
  3. Product allocation planning data
  4. Assign Product to product allocation 

I have even enabled PAL in CBC. But still, during the sales Order Processing, no object appears. 

In Material Master, I have used SR as a check for availability. 

Need Help here.

entry.title: Error de mensaje 75 IDoc en proceso de cola
entry.content:
Buen día Estimados

Estamos trabajando en enviar Idocs en una Cola y desde ECC a S4HANA, con documentos Contable FIDCC2 y estos quedan en estados 75 al llegar a su destino, 

se esta trabajando con MF we85 CONSTANT:EDIQUEUE para que los IDocs viajen de manera eficiente pero vemos que cuando falla un Idocs la cola se detiene y los deja en ese estado 75

 tambien intentamos con MF FIRST_16_OF_MESTYP, pero este los enviara los idocs y los deja en estado 75 en el destino, tampoco los IDocs se visualizan en las Tx SMQ1 o SMQ2,

las conexiones del sistema emisor

las conexiones del sistema receptor

si hay alguna forma de que se pueda solucionar este error les agradeceria mucho una solucion.

Gracias por tanto.

entry.title: Change Number is ignored for class type 300 in S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
When doing changes for class 300 with changenumber we receive the error ' Change Number ignored for class type 300'

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Change Number is ignored for class type 300 in S/4HAN...
entry.content:
The issue is documented in the KBA 2662172 

 

For the Class type 300 the customizing "ECH Time" is not set in S4HANA Cloud for object MARA.

 

The resolution in the KBA 2662172 is applicable to S4HANA Onpremise only.

 

There is no SSCUI / Expert Configuration available to enable this customizing in S4HANA Cloud .

entry.title: Re: In C4C utilities, Do we have functionlaity to know th...
entry.content:
Audit Trail in C4C will not cover all standard scenarios. Refer the note -

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3041390

For supported scenarios

entry.title: Re: Resize of Group Tree does not work anymore
entry.content: Forget my comment found the release date in the other Forum Question.

entry.title: SAP Datasphere - SAP Cloud ALM data collection and alerts
entry.content:
Hi.

During configuration of my tenant of SAP Datasphere I followed the guide.

The system now is visible in health monitoring and collect the data.

But when try to insert the new alert for example, to failed tasks, in Integration & Exception Monitoring my system don't view any data: "Not Configured"

If I choose "edit configuration" in "monitoring" sections I don't view any entries... it is normal?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere - SAP Cloud ALM data collection and al...
entry.content:
You shared this link in your description text.

If you examine the top of that page, it lists the SAP Cloud ALM for Operations use cases that are currently supported for SAP Datasphere. The outline of the This is a convention followed by all of the product pages in our setup guides list. Integration & Exception Monitoring is not listed under SAP Datasphere, so it is not supported and you cannot enable data collection or configure events. 

More information on how to check supported products and metrics can be found in this KBA: 3426195 - What SAP Cloud ALM monitoring and metrics are available for my SAP product?

If you wish for Integration & Exception Monitoring to support SAP Datasphere, please log a request (or vote on an existing one) using our Customer Influence campaign. More information can be found in this KBA: 3391676 - SAP Continuous Influence campaigns for SAP Cloud ALM

entry.title: Re: Approval Strategy for Sales Order
entry.content: configure Status profile

entry.title: Re: Create Outbound Delivery in EWM: 2025 Edition
entry.content:
@DaniilThanks for the comment! By the message text I meant what we have in the good old BAPI return type table. It contains not just the system fields (ID, number, etc.) but also the Text field with the human-readable formatted message text. The blog text has only a few code fragments, full code shows how I've done it:

DATA(messages) = message_box->get_messages_with_no( ).
cl_demo_output=>display( messages ).

After executing this, I was surprised not to see the formatted text. But maybe I missed something.

entry.title: Analysing ABAP performance by Call Stack
entry.content:
In this blog post, I’ll walk you through the process of performing a root cause analysis for performance bottlenecks in ABAP applications. We'll focus specifically on how to evaluate the ABAP application call stack to identify the underlying issues that may be impacting system performance. By understanding the structure and behavior of the call stack, you can pinpoint inefficient code, redundant processing, or problematic custom developments — ultimately leading to more optimized and responsive SAP systems.


1. ABAP stack for running user session

The call stack is a valuable tool for root cause analysis in ABAP applications. It provides the sequence of the class methods, subroutines, or function modules that lead to an error or issue, starting with the current program and going back to the initial starting point. This is very important to understand where the problem begins and who the responsible developer is.

 Example: The application SAPLZHK01 is responsible for the NRIV lock wait.

->  View the call stack in the work process info.
Select a work process and then execute the WP-info in the 'Go To' menu or by double-clicking on it in the ALV.

-> To generate a call stack in work process diagnostic file 
Execute the write stack in SM50 in the Administration -> Work Process -> Write Stack menu.

-> Server Snapshot
Server snapshot (also known as SAP Kernel snapshot) is a feature available since SAP NetWeaver ABAP Kernel 740. It is a concept of the NetWeaver ABAP application server which aims to save the most important system information automatically when the system is in abnormal situations. It saves the SM21 system log, ABAP stack, C-STACK,  and developer trace files in a single ZIP file. It can also be triggered manually via SM50 menu Administration -> Snapshot -> Create

-> Trigger the snapshot from the operating system using the sapcontrol command

Tools for the offline analysis

- SAP Microsoft Management Console 
- Kernel snapshot analyzer

-> Server Snapshot
The server snapshot file can be accessed via the transaction code SNAPSHOTS or SM50 menu Administration -> Snapshot -> Administration. The snapshot file can be read directly in SNAPSHOTS, or offline analysis tools like SAP MMC and Kernel Snapshot Analyzer

-> ST12 ABAP trace

 

2. ABAP stack of the user session in the past

Use /SDF/SMON to extract ABAP stack of technical issues in the past.

SMON (tx /SDF/SMON) is to analyze past performance problems or to monitor the system. To use SMON for analysis, it must be scheduled first before the desired analysis period or already running when the issue occurred.

SMON is the successor to /SDF/MON and collects additional data including the ABAP stack. SMON is available as of Software Component ST-PI Release 740 Service Pack 0002

How to Schedule SMON: SAP Note 2651881 - How to configure SMON for performance monitoring and analysis.

The monitoring data is stored mainly in DB tables /SDF/SMON_HEADER and /SDF/SMON_WPINFO for the last 3 days and then stored to /SDF/SMON in the compressed format during the predefined retention period. The call stack information is stored in the DB table /SDF/SMON_STACK and /SDF/SMON_STACKH

Typical SMON daily scheduling:

Execute the transaction /SDF/SMON and check whether the SMON was running in the analysis period in question 

The aggregated work process overview

Use case examples
- Analysis of the delay of a batch job already finished
- Find the most expensive operation that led system’s high load in the past

Find the Call Stack in SMON

Display SM50 data with the filter ‘Only records with Call stack’

Direct SMON DB table access example

SMON DB tables

Limitation 

Example
An intermittent performance issue in S4/HANA when calling the HTTP RFC destination of the SAP ETAX BTP solution.

The work process overview history data in /SDF/SMON offers enhanced clarity regarding the issue : The work process was executing the same batch job, and it was on SAPLHTTP_RUNTIME most of time and the call stack was the same for all snapshots. However, it was not a ‘hanging’ situation for a single HTTP call because the work process performed different actions between snapshots

Example 
An intermittent data inconsistency issue within CO and SD components. The only technical finding was that when the issue occurred, the V1 423 message (an A type message) appeared in the system log while executing CO BAPI functions. However, the specific call stack linking these events remained unidentified.

Identifying the call stack :
- Find the ABAP include program that generate that specific message using SE91 where used list
- List up date, time, instance name and the work process number in the syslog
- Search available /SDF/SMON call stack based on the information collected

 

Example
Analyzing issues that happened in the past - an unusual long run time of a particular batch job. 

Root cause analysis using the /SDF/MON snapshot data
- Used /SDF/MON because /SDF/SMON was not available in the system (Call stack is not available in /SDF/MON)
- Analyze the SM50 activity data using the instance name and the work process number that ran the batch job
- Used work process activity aggregation to identify the root cause of the performance issue

Example
Analyzing issues that happened in the past 
ENQUEUE performance issue in the past, the issue was more than 2000 work processes were stuck at ABAP program SAPLSENA waiting for the response from the standalone ENQUEUE server.

entry.title: Large Volume of Data Replication – SAP S/4HANA to SAP Commerce Cloud (Hybris): Hotfolders vs. SCPI?
entry.content: For a large volume of product master data — approximately 5 million records — with daily inventory and pricing updates, we are evaluating for the best replication/integration (SAP to Hybris) approach in terms of performance, throughput, and resource utilization. In this case, which option would be more suitable: via Hotfolders (Azure Storage) or SAP Cloud Platform Integration (SCPI)?

entry.title: User-Defined Criteria in Supplier Evaluation – PPM Header and Missing Entries
entry.content:
Hello,

I am using Supplier Evaluation in SAP S/4HANA (not public cloud) and have defined PPM as a user-defined criterion.

However, the header in the app "Supplier Evaluation by User-Defined Criteria" still shows the default name and does not update to reflect "PPM". How can I change "Criteria 6" to "PPM" and delete the rest? 

Additionally, only 2 suppliers are displayed in the app – do I need to schedule a background job or anything else to get the full list? It should have a lot more entrys for 2024 which are not shown - No filter applicated 

And the PPM Score is also not shown in the Overall Supplier Evaluation App F2019. 

 

Thanks in advance for your help!

entry.title: Re: Is Data extraction from SAP SF-ICM to SAP datasphere...
entry.content:
I am not sure if you can. SF has no ODP/ODQ nor CDS based deltas. You need to store the delta somewhere. 

I did a generic like delta extraction as a workaround for this. 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/sap-datasphere-delta-extraction/ba-p/13699391

entry.title: Re: 'IAM APP' does not appear in eclipse

entry.title: Re: 'IAM APP' does not appear in eclipse

Something I noticed is that it seems you have at least 2 projects in Eclipse

  • TRL_EN (which I understand being the ABAP environment in BTP where you want to create the IAM App)
  • S4H_200_DEVFIORI272_EN (which I guess is an on premise environment not related with this) 

See the image you provided the second project is selected.

entry.title: How to compare key figure value with null value with in sac planning data action script
entry.content:
Hello,

I have two key figures Z_SALES1, Z_SALES2 along with other dimensions selections. The key figure Z_SALES2 value will not enter always by user. It should copy from Z_SALES1 value to ZSALES2 if user does not enter Z_SALES2 key figure or it is ‘zero’

I tried the below code at Data Action getting error(The different types can't be compared).
Only Zero (0) allowing comparing,  but not considering double quats (“”) or single quats (‘’) to compare null value.

IF RESULTLOOKUP ([d/Measures] = "Z_SALES2", [d/ZCCODE] = %CCODE_Input%) = 0 or “ ” THEN
DATA([d/Measures] = "Z_SALES2", [d/ZCCODE] = %CCODE_Input%) =
RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = "public.Budget", [d/ZCCODE] = %CCODE_Input%,  [d/Measures] = "Z_SALES1")
ENDIF

How to compare if key figure value null value. Please help.

Regards,
Vasu

entry.title: AI in Action: New mission available to enhance SAP Signavio with Machine Learning
entry.content:
In today's rapidly evolving business landscape, organizations are continually seeking innovative ways to enhance operational efficiency and stay ahead of the competition. One groundbreaking approach is the integration of machine learning (ML) with process mining, a strategy that transforms static data into dynamic, predictive insights. SAP Signavio Process Intelligence, in conjunction with SAP Build, offers a robust platform to achieve this integration seamlessly.

Unlocking Predictive Process Management

Traditionally, process mining involves analyzing historical event logs to understand past performance. While this retrospective analysis is valuable, it doesn't provide foresight into future outcomes. By embedding ML algorithms into your process mining activities, you can predict future events, identify potential bottlenecks before they occur, and make data-driven decisions that proactively enhance process efficiency.

Practical Applications

Consider the challenge of predicting sales order delivery times. By analyzing historical event logs, ML models can forecast delivery dates based on factors like order size, product type, and current workload. These predictions, when fed back into SAP Signavio Process Intelligence, offer real-time insights, enabling better planning and improved customer satisfaction.

Another application is in customer support. By examining event logs, ML can predict customer satisfaction levels with support tickets, considering variables such as resolution speed and communication quality. This allows support teams to proactively address issues, enhancing the overall customer experience.

New mission OUT NOW

We are excited to announce a new mission that showcases how you can realize these use cases using SAP Signavio in combination with SAP Build.

https://developers.sap.com/mission.signavio-pi-and-sap-build.html 

Successfully implement the mission and earn a cool badge as a reward.

 

It will highlight your success of combining multiple SAP solutions and showcase your ability to tackle new topics and realize value from it!

Seamless Integration with SAP Build

SAP Build, equipped with Python and Jupyter capabilities, serves as the bridge between raw data and actionable insights. It enables the extraction of event log data from SAP Signavio Process Intelligence, the application of ML algorithms, and the reintegration of enriched data back into the system. This seamless workflow ensures that your process management system evolves from reactive to proactive, continually learning and adapting to new data.

Getting Started

Embarking on this transformative journey involves a few key steps:

  1. Activate APIs: Enable the OData and Ingestion APIs in SAP Signavio Process Intelligence to facilitate data movement.
  2. Data Extraction: Utilize SAP Build to extract event log data via the OData API.
  3. Apply Machine Learning: Develop and train ML models within SAP Build to analyze and predict outcomes based on your data.
  4. Data Reintegration: Push the enriched, predictive data back into SAP Signavio Process Intelligence through the Ingestion API.

    For a comprehensive, step-by-step guide on implementing this integration, refer to the mission above.

    By harnessing the combined power of SAP Signavio Process Intelligence and SAP Build, your organization can transition to a predictive process management paradigm, driving efficiency, foresight, and a competitive edge in your industry.

entry.title: SD_COND_ACCESS - performance S/4 vs ECC
entry.content:
Hi All,

After migrating to S/4 we face performance issue with Pricing routine - SD_COND_ACCESS. For example in calculating Salary components - about 1200 times calling SD_COND_ACCESS which takes (now about 90 seconds were it was about 20-30 in ECC). Also other transactions like DP91 / VF04 / VK12  are much slower compared to ECC. Does anybody have similar experience S/4 <-> ECC ?   Some SAP note's say not user table buffers - but that made it even worse.  Could program CONDITION_PRESTEP_ANALYZE help - can you just run it for everything ?  Any other  recommendations ?

Regards,

Jeroen Cosijnse

Do you have any idea, if there is some standard customizing how to solve this scenario?

Thans for any idea.

Radek

entry.title: Employer's Business or Organization Address Section 2 of Form I-9
entry.content:
Hello,

In SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding, the Employer’s Business or Organization Address in Section 2 of Form I-9 currently defaults to the address of the legal entity (corporate headquarters).

Is there a way to override this logic so the field dynamically defaults to the employee’s assigned work location instead?

Can this be achieved through a configuration or setting within the US Compliance Form I-9, or would a custom form be required?

Thanks in advance for any guidance.

entry.title: Re: Use of MRP-Relevant Dep. Requirements in MRP 4 view
entry.content:
Hello

You can create a storage location MRP area and define MRP type ND for the component that should be excluded from planning. In the Bill of Material, you can define this specific storage location for the component that should not be planned. 

Regards,
Caetano

entry.title: SuccessFactors & Docusign Integration - Offer Letters
entry.content:

Noteworthy, the image appears on the offer prior to selecting that step.

entry.title: Standard Audit File for Tax Purpose - SAFT Includes Troubleshooting
entry.content:
The following blog provides very crucial information about troubleshooting of SAF-T Romania which includes as well End-to-End troubleshooting process on SAP S/4HANA on Promise and Private Cloud.

 

  1. FAQs for SAF-T Romania Solution: Discover answers to your frequently asked questions about the SAF-T Romania solution for both SAP ERP (ABAP-based) and SAP S/4HANA (Fiori-based) systems. Gain insights into compliance and functionality specifics to stay informed and streamlined.

  2. Implementing SAF-T Romania Solution on SAP ERP: End-to-End Troubleshooting: Explore a comprehensive guide on implementing the SAF-T Romania solution within the SAF-T New Framework (SAFTN) in SAP ERP. Learn about the end-to-end process involving extraction and generation, ensuring seamless compliance with ANAF's Information Statement D406.

  3. Implementing SAF-T Romania Solution on SAP S/4HANA On Premise/Private Cloud: End-to-End Troubleshooting: Dive into the implementation and troubleshooting process for the SAF-T Romania solution on SAP S/4HANA systems. Understand how to use the Fiori-based Statutory Reporting framework to generate the SAF-T file (Information Statement D406) accurately and efficiently.

entry.title: Re: Filtering nested propery in API
entry.content: You are correct, in the question of the original poster it would be $filter=account.name eq 'Arral Energy US'

entry.title: Re: Rebilling of ordered items via customer project
entry.content:
Hello @maik1860 ,

Thank you for the update — I’m glad to hear that my previous input was helpful. Regarding the issue where the amount is not recognised as revenue and therefore cannot be invoiced, I’ve reviewed the screenshot you provided. This error typically occurs when the cost element or account assignment isn’t correctly configured for revenue recognition within the billing process. Here are a few things you might consider check:

  • Account Assignment – Ensure the supplier invoice is posted with a revenue-relevant account assignment.
  • Cost Element Settings – Verify that the cost element is marked as relevant for billing and mapped correctly in the revenue recognition settings.
  • Project Billing Profile – Confirm that the billing profile assigned to the project includes the appropriate settings for recognizing revenue from supplier invoices.
  • Error Message Details – If possible, please share the exact text of the error message to help further narrow down the issue.

Let me know once you've reviewed these areas, or feel free to share more details if the problem persists. I’ll be happy to assist further.

Best regards,
Chuma

entry.title: Surfing the GOSI Update: From Concept to Delivered Solution
entry.content:
Riding the Wave of Reform

Last year, we introduced you to a new chapter in Saudi Arabia’s social insurance reform — a bold initiative by GOSI to unify pension systems and gradually raise retirement age and contribution rates. At the time, we were still shaping our response. But today, the solution is no longer on the horizon — it’s live.

This blog highlights what has changed since our first post what SAP has delivered, and what’s next on the compliance roadmap. 

What Changed Since Our Last Blog?

In our earlier blog, we introduced the law’s intent and outlined three employee groups based on age and GOSI contribution history. Back then, the solution was still in discussion.

Since then, several assumptions have been simplified:

  • No new fields are needed for first contribution tracking.
  • No contribution proration is required between July 1–2 and July 3 — the effective date for the new SI group (SN) was adjusted to July 1st.
  • A combination of popup messages and business rules now guides users in assigning the correct SI Group — without requiring external GOSI data.

What Has SAP Delivered?

1. Effective Date Adjustment

To ensure calculation consistency, the effective date for the SN contribution percentage is now July 1st (2025–2028), avoiding monthly proration.

2. SI Group Assignment Logic (SAP Payroll)

Infotype 3252 now defaults the SI Group based on the employee’s Hijri date of birth and whether they contributed to GOSI before July 3, 2024.

If the employee is under 50 Hijri, a popup prompts the user: i.e. SU is selected if the answer is Yes while SN is selected if No

On HR Renewal mashup screens, a warning message appears instead of the popup.

3. EC and ECP Handling (Business Rule)

For customers using Employee Central or Employee Central Payroll, a Business Rule in Global Info raises a warning when the SI Group is entered manually. This ensures users follow the same classification logic applied in SAP Payroll.

4. SANED Retirement Handling – With Flexibility

SAP now supports automatically stopping SANED contributions once the employee reaches retirement age. This reflects real-life cases where the employee continues working — often due to expertise or business needs.

In such scenarios, the employer may choose to continue the GOSI annuity contribution (basic fund) by checking the “No Age Check” flag in Infotype 3252. The system continues the annuity and excludes SANED automatically, maintaining compliance without manual steps.

What’s on the Horizon?

As a continuation of GOSI modernization, a new business-to-authority API is now available from GOSI to provide the contribution percentage per employee. This may eliminate the need for maintaining contribution parameters manually.

SAP is currently evaluating this integration offering for potential inclusion in future releases.

| Please refer to the SAP disclaimer regarding forward-looking features.

In parallel, we’re also preparing a new blog covering the recent updates to the Saudi Labor Law Executive Regulations. This will showcase recommended solution designs (not standard delivery) to help manage new legal expectations around employee sanctions, probation, notice periods, and leave policies using extensibility tools like MDF and business rules. Stay tuned!

Want the Technical Details?

We’ve documented the full solution — including SAP Notes, system behavior, and rule logic — in this KBA:

New Social Insurance System – Master Note KBA

entry.title: Re: Learning Hub Partner Edition - Four attempts for cert...
entry.content:
Hello,
It is per user that you get 4 certification attempts and you cannot share them across multiple users. I even think that the stay current assessments aren't taken into those 4 attempts and they count for new certifications. However, you need a learning hub license to take the stay current assessments.

Kind regards,

Robin

entry.title: Re: Learning Hub Partner Edition - Four attempts for cert...
entry.content:
Hi there,

it is 4 attempts per user.

regards
Faraz

entry.title: Re: Background Creation of Production versions
entry.content:
Hello

You are right, report CS_BOM_PRODVER_MIGRATION02 only creates production versions based on BOMs and routings that are valid in the current date. Perhaps you can create your own version of the report and search for additional BOMs and routings in the future. 

Regards,
Caetano

entry.title: Re: Product Allocation Not appreaing in Availability chec...
entry.content:
Hello @ankitdeshwal 

Thanks for sharing your setup details.

It seems you’ve completed the main PAL configuration steps, but no allocation appears likely due to one or more of these reasons:

  • Product Allocation profile may not be assigned to the correct sales area in Manage Product Allocation Sequences.
  • Planning data might be missing or not cover the sales order’s material, plant, or period.
  • Availability check indicator SR must have PAL activated in the availability check settings for the sales area.
  • The sales order item category might not support product allocation.
  • PAL may not be fully activated in Central Business Configuration (CBC).

Please check error logs or try the Product Allocation Simulation app. 

Let me know if you need further assistance

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: FSCM CM: Net Value of the itens from MM origin applic...
entry.content: Thanks for you answer. I actualy found an request in SAP influence customer. I hope it'll be considered in the next release. ..."https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/339011"... BR

entry.title: Re: Multiple Billing Document Attachments in Single Email...
entry.content:
Hello @dlindsey 

In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, it is not possible by default to group multiple billing documents (PDFs) into a single email per customer. The system sends one email per billing document.

Options:

  • Manual workaround: Download PDFs and email them manually.
  • Custom solution: Use SAP BTP/CPI to build a middleware for grouping and sending.
  • Alternative channels: Use EDI or IDocs instead of email to reduce volume.

For automation, custom development or a request to SAP Support would be needed.

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: SAP Screen Personas Global JavaScript Library for Similar Transactions
entry.content:
With global scripts, you don't use session.utils.include
You can review the corresponding unit of the Personas course at Reusing scripts across multiple flavors
If you need to pass data to such a script, that's possible using session.utils.put and then reading this in the global script via session.utils.get

entry.title: Re: How to fix time out issues where tracking won't allow...
entry.content: @Learning_Discovery, here you can find additional details to be verified: Common Problems and Solutions for SCORM 1.2 and AICC Content. Furthermore, if the user's computer enters sleep/suspend mode, the internet connection will potentially terminate, leading to the lack of ping that keeps the session active, causing the logout.

entry.title: Re: How to fix time out issues where tracking won't allow...
entry.content: Hey thanks for that - I'll check out that document. But if there is a lack of ping (if the computer goes to sleep/suspend) and the session remains "open" is there a way around it? Like an automatic logout of the seesion, rather than letting the user carry on as if nothing happened? (I suspect this happens since user's computer likely buffers the remaining course content...)

entry.title: Re: How to Get EWay Bill PDF in SAP S/4 Hana Public Edition
entry.content:
Hello @SaiBhargav396 

In SAP S/4HANA Public Edition, the EDocument Cockpit does not offer a standard option to print the E-Way Bill PDF.
Summary:

  • E-Way Bill data is generated and tracked in the EDocument Cockpit.
  • PDF printing is not available directly in SAP.
  • The official PDF must be downloaded from the NIC portal.
  • Custom development (e.g., via SAP BTP) is required to print E-Way Bills from SAP.

For official use, download from NIC. For internal use, consider custom printing logic if necessary.

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: How do I upload an Excel File to Enable Now
entry.content: How do I upload an excel file in Enable Now so that is publishable to the Learner view?

entry.title: Re: Reactive maintenance order (YA01) creation without no...
entry.content:
Hello @Esnowy 

you cannot create a YA01 order without an associated notification in your current SAP Public Cloud setup, because the "Header Notification" is mandatory.

Thank you and kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Customer Vendor Integration (CVI) during S4 HANA Conversion (Brown Field Implementation)
entry.content:
Hello,

I am carrying out a conversion, but the CVI_PRECHK does not return errors, even though there are errors in the database. In debug mode, the errors appear in the error table, but not in the result of PRECHK. I have already consulted some notes, but I was not successful.

 

Best regards,

Vanessa Joao

entry.title: bapi_po_create1 : passing standard table field J_1BNBM into bapi
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,
I have a field J_1BNBM in EKPO table which is getting field with control code.
There is validaion in po creation ME21N using method IF_EX_ME_PROCESS_PO_CUST~PROCESS_ITEM to make the field as mandatory due to which I am getting the
same error while using the BAPI_PO_CREATE1.
However, there is no field available  in line item of BAPI_PO_CREATE1 of related to J_1BNBM or control code.
The bapi will only post the PO if I could able to pass control_code(J_1BNBM)  using the same BAPI.
I thought of using as well as tried to achieve it through extension . But extension is for only customer add on fields .

J_1BNBM is standard field in EKPO and not customer field.

How to resolve this issue as I am at crossroads now ?
